title: Variables, Expressions, Equations, and Practical Formulas · Mastery Check
summary: Build reusable cost, distance, energy, and capacity formulas from stakeholder requirements. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a formula-driven planning calculator. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define variables with units and domains rather than treating letters as unlabeled blanks.
Substitute values into expressions with grouping, units, and intermediate reasonableness checks.
Solve one-variable linear equations while preserving equality at every transformation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Inequality Constraints for a formula-driven planning calculator.
steps.why: The Inequality Constraints result for a formula-driven planning calculator is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model limits and feasible ranges with inequalities, including sign reversal under negative scaling.

content.text: Before calculating Inequality Constraints, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a formula-driven planning calculator.
